data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay) for the DOCS-ONLY plan "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern": CLEAN — no T8 findings.

The overlay applies at one remove: this plan DOCUMENTS an LLM sub-system (the three/four-pass gate + the Pass-4 affirmative coach), it does not DEFINE new prompts/schemas/enums. T8's task here is to confirm every schema/enum/protocol the docs must transcribe resolves to a concrete, fully-specified vocabulary an author can faithfully document (an under-specified referent would make AC1/AC3 unsatisfiable). I tool-grounded each referent against docs/plan-review-gate.md + the referenced tickets; all resolve.

(a) No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is undefined.
- The move registry the docs must cover is fully enumerated — moves 1-9,11,12 with locked `{subject}` templates at docs/plan-review-gate.md:112-124, realized in orchestrator.MOVE_REGISTRY; project-extension contract `{move_id: {name, template}}` via .rebar/plan_review_moves.json documented at :126-130.
- The config surface (risk posture, chunking, budget caps, per-criterion blocking, enable/disable) has a concrete documented vocabulary: verify.require_plan_review_for_claim (:178-197), block_threshold/posture, the advisory cap (:132-139).
- The reference-by-id contract names its owner: ca03 mints the content-fingerprint finding id (sole owner), this coach CONSUMES it, db7b's sidecar records it (75a9 §Caller-visible finding id contract) — non-circular, verified against the tickets.

(b) Decision-rule co-located with its schema: Pass-3 decision math sits beside the validity/impact/priority schema (:96-103); the C1 SUBJECT VALIDATOR (REJECT⇒no-coaching) sits beside the move-render rule (:128-130).

(c) No ambiguous counter/state-increment: there are no counters in the docs surface; cap/block ownership is explicit (Pass-3 owns the top-N cap + block decision; "the cap can never weaken the block decision", :136-139).

(d) Fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step IS specified — and is itself a documented topic: fail-open is fully enumerated at :199-210 (unsupported stack / missing tool / parse / timeout → abstain-as-PASS; LLM unavailable → DET-only; claim gate fails closed when signing is unavailable). The coach's own best-effort drop (rejected subject / no-move ⇒ no coaching) is specified in 75a9.

(e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/docs exist: the cross-referenced workflow-engine usage doc is 6f2d WS-DOC (OPEN epic; canonical docs/workflow-engine.md is its pending deliverable), and 75a9 (the Pass-4 coach) is CLOSED/specified. The "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored deterministic template, not LLM judgment — a positive-by-construction mechanism with no load-bearing negative instruction.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes): no undefined-but-referenced enum/protocol (every documented referent resolves to a concrete vocabulary in docs/plan-review-gate.md + the tickets, tool-verified); decision-rule co-located with its schema; no ambiguous counter/state placement; an explicit best-effort fallback for failed/incomplete sub-steps (fail-open is itself one of the documented topics); referenced agents/docs (6f2d, 75a9, ca03, db7b) exist and the coaching property is template-baked.

(Non-T8 note for the consistency/correctness pass, not a structural gap: the plan's "What"/AC name a "global enable/disable kill-switch", but the canonical doc states one is NOT needed — "Turning it off is the rollback … no kill-switch needed", :180. The config vocabulary IS defined; the plan and the doc merely disagree on the kill-switch framing. That is a doc-content reconciliation, not an undefined-enum/missing-protocol/ambiguous-counter/unspecified-fallback gap, so it does not rise to a T8 finding.)

Per the T8 ANTI-FP clause: a well-specified plan whose referents are tool-confirmed against the canonical doc and code is a PASS. No structural gap is provable — emitting no T8 finding is correct.
